Streaming: The Come Up is the debut mixtape by rapper J. Cole, released on May 4, 2007 and hosted by DJ OnPoint. On November 19, 2024, the mixtape was re-released onto streaming platforms, accompanying the first episode release of Cole's audio podcast series Inevitable, which focused on this mixtape.

Streaming: The Warm Up is the second official mixtape from rapper J. Cole, released on June 15th, 2009. This album is the turning point for J. Cole since the lead single for this album, "Lights Please," got the attention of Jay-Z, who later signed him to Roc Nation. On November 22, 2024, the mixtape was re-released onto streaming platforms, accompanying the third episode release of Cole's audio podcast series Inevitable, which focused on this mixtape.

Streaming: Cole World: The Sideline Story is the debut studio album by rapper J. Cole, released on September 27th, 2011.

Name:
Who Datt Pt. 2
(feat. Spencer Datt & Childish Gambino)
(Who Dat (Remix))
Notes: Remix of "Who Dat" with a feature from Spencer Datt, who was a huge J. Cole fan, with his own verse and Childish Gambino. Peter Rosenberg, Datt's brother-in-law, had revealed that Datt was an aspiring rapper who was killed in a car accident a month after he proposed to Rosenberg's sister. He later put the remix together in his honor and persuaded two of Datt's favorite rappers, J. Cole and Childish Gambino, to record new verses for him. The song was later released on September 7th, 2011 on What's Poppin' Volume One, made by Peter.

Name: ⭐ everybody dies
Notes: Single released on December 5th, 2016, meant to be on "4 Your Eyez Only" but didn't fit the vibe so it got omitted. This song has J. Cole taking aim at rappers using "Lil" in their stage names on the track, which had people speculating that this was directed at Lil Yachty and Lil Uzi Vert. Was also supposed to be on "The Fall Off" before getting scrapped and released as a loosie single.

Name:
⭐ False Prophets
(prod. Freddie Joachim)
(Waves)
Notes: Single released on December 5th, 2016, meant to be on "4 Your Eyez Only" but didn't fit the vibe so it got omitted. This song was also planned to be on The Fall Off, which would've come out in 2020. The instrumental to this song comes from "Waves" by Joey Badass. According to Badass, he gave J. Cole "his blessing" after Cole reached out and played the song for him. Was also supposed to be on "The Fall Off" before getting scrapped and released as a loosie single.
